entities.name: V (Vrijen)

entities.name: V's co-founder
entities.context: Unnamed co-founder with background in college admissions and career coaching, specializing in narrative design

entities.name: Jared Dunn
entities.context: Fictional example profile used to illustrate Careerspan—represents talented people who are poor at self-advocacy

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: Company built around structured self-reflection to help candidates articulate their value

entities.name: Careerspan core thesis
entities.context: The real problem is not formatting resumes but helping people know what to say about themselves

entities.name: Narrative design
entities.context: Specialization of founders from college admissions and career coaching background

entities.name: Structured self-reflection
entities.context: Methodology Careerspan is built around—candidates answer deep, guided questions about experiences

entities.name: Narrative positioning statement
entities.context: Generated output describing candidate identity (e.g., 'a product manager with a designer's brain and a researcher's discipline')

entities.name: Semantic alignment
entities.context: Approach to rewriting resumes to align with specific roles rather than just keyword-match

entities.name: Job decomposition
entities.context: Process of breaking down a job into responsibilities and skills for candidate scoring

entities.name: Candidate scoring
entities.context: Scoring candidates on direct and transferable evidence for each dimension of a role

entities.name: Guided questions
entities.context: Deep questions (typed or spoken) about specific experiences to capture rich stories
